original_text: "Made or received digital payment: ~35%" -- cited in the challenge brief's Global Findex 2024 summary but never entered as a data row. Added here because USG_DIGITAL_PAYMENT is one of the two required forecast targets (Usage pillar) and had zero observations without this addition.
notes: Confidence set to 'medium' rather than 'high' because the brief states this as an approximate figure (~35%), not an exact published number. This is also the ONLY observation for this indicator -- a trend line cannot be fit from one point; see forecasting.py's single-point fallback and the methodology note's limitations section.

original_text: Telebirr had ~3.5yrs runway by the 2024 survey vs M-Pesa's ~1.3yrs, and reached 54.8M registered users by mid-2025 (REC_0021). Attributes the majority of the +4.75pp mobile-money-account growth 2021-2024 to Telebirr's scale-up.
notes: Paired with IMP_0002; the split between Telebirr/M-Pesa is a modeling assumption, not a direct measurement -- validate against quarterly registration data if available.

source_name: Derived: REC_0007/REC_0008 residual + REC_0023 (10.8M M-Pesa users, 2024)
original_text: Remaining share of the 2021-2024 MM-account growth not attributed to Telebirr; consistent with M-Pesa's smaller, newer user base.
notes: Complements IMP_0001. Bucketed medium rather than high given the smaller/newer base -- true baseline for a relative-% calc is not directly observable.

original_text: A mobile-money registration only lifts Findex 'account ownership' when the person was previously unbanked. Market-nuance data shows mobile-money-only users are rare (~0.5%), so most Telebirr registrants already had bank accounts -- capping pass-through to the Access pillar. 0.8pp / 46% baseline = ~1.7% relative change.
notes: Key mechanism explaining why 65M+ mobile money accounts produced only +3pp headline Access growth. Marked 'indirect' (relationship_type) since it works through an unbanked-conversion mechanism, not a direct registration count.

source_name: Same logic as IMP_0003, scaled to M-Pesa's smaller base
original_text: 0.3pp / 46% baseline = ~0.65% relative change -- below the 1% negligible threshold.

source_name: REC_0021/REC_0022 (54.8M users, 2.38T ETB txn value FY24/25)
original_text: Telebirr is the dominant digital payment rail in Ethiopia by both users and transaction value; treated as primary driver of the ~35% digital payment adoption figure reported for 2024.
notes: No pre-Telebirr digital-payment baseline exists in the dataset to compute a clean relative %, so 'high' reflects Telebirr's outsized share of the 2024 total rather than a before/after ratio.

source_name: General e-KYC / digital-ID financial-inclusion literature (e.g. India Aadhaar-linked account opening)
original_text: Digital ID simplifies KYC for account opening. Ethiopia's rollout is still early (12-15M of a 90M-by-2028 target per REC_0013/REC_0014), so this is a forward-looking assumption for 2026-2028, not something visible in the 2024 Findex round.
notes: 'enabling' rather than 'direct' -- Fayda creates the conditions for easier account opening, it doesn't open accounts itself. Key forecast-scenario lever.

original_text: Digital ID simplifies e-KYC onboarding to digital payment apps, not just bank accounts.

source_name: No direct comparable identified -- theoretical inference only
original_text: FX liberalization creates incentive to move remittance/forex flows onto licensed digital channels rather than the parallel market. Direction is plausible but magnitude is genuinely uncertain.
notes: Lowest-confidence link in the table -- treat as a sensitivity-test input, not a core assumption.

source_name: Standard telecom-competition effect on subscriber growth
original_text: Market entry of a second major operator typically accelerates mobile subscription growth through price/coverage competition.
notes: Also functions as an 'enabling' precursor to M-Pesa uptake, but the direct effect modeled here is on ACC_MOBILE_PEN specifically.

source_name: General pattern from mobile-money interoperability rollouts in other African markets
original_text: Interoperability launches elsewhere have been associated with double-digit percentage jumps in P2P transaction volume as network effects widen beyond a single platform's user base.
notes: Launched Oct 2025 -- no Ethiopia post-period data exists yet; revisit once FY2025/26 EthSwitch figures are available. Bucketed 'medium' since the 15% estimate sits at the high/medium boundary, not clearly above it.

original_text: Interoperability increases the utility of an M-Pesa wallet for counterparties on the Telebirr network, plausibly lifting the 90-day active-user rate (REC_0024/REC_0025).

source_name: Instant/real-time payment rail precedents (e.g. India UPI, Brazil Pix -- general pattern, not Ethiopia-specific figures)
original_text: Real-time payment infrastructure has historically accelerated digital payment adoption in other markets. EthioPay launched Dec 2025, so this is purely a 2026-2027 forecast assumption.
notes: Pair with IMP_0007/IMP_0008 as the main 'forward-looking, unvalidated' cluster for the uncertainty writeup.

original_text: Instant payment systems typically shift higher-value transactions onto digital rails, raising transaction value more than transaction count.

A multi-year national coordinating strategy (2021-2025) rather than a discrete intervention -- it acts through the other events in this table (Telebirr, Fayda, etc.), not as an independent step-change.
notes: Recommend modeling as a period/regime dummy variable rather than assigning it its own point-magnitude effect, to avoid double-counting with IMP_0001-0004. 'mixed' direction and 'negligible' magnitude reflect that its isolable, standalone effect (net of the events it enabled) is not meaningfully separable.

original_text: Tariff increases typically dampen new-user acquisition momentum in following quarters rather than shrinking the existing base.
notes: Recent event (Dec 2025) -- treat as a near-term forecast headwind for 2026.
